fault_hypothesis: On line 68, `adjusted_now` is computed as `now - _CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE` (subtracting the tolerance), but it should be `now + _CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE` (or the comparison on line 73 uses `>=` instead of `>`). The result is that when `now == expires_at`, `adjusted_now < expires_at`, so the entry is incorrectly returned instead of treated as expired.

constraints.intent: adjusted_now should be >= now (tolerance added, not subtracted) so that boundary cases are correctly treated as expired
After computing adjusted_now, it must be at least as large as now (clock-skew tolerance should be added, not subtracted)
Before using adjusted_now in the expiry comparison, verify it is not less than now (which would cause boundary entries to be incorrectly returned)
If we reach the return value branch, adjusted_now must be strictly less than expires_at (not equal), confirming the entry is truly not expired
